name: TAO Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether continuation becomes tradable only after a breakout retests and holds rather than on the breakout candle itself. The edge is the delayed retest hold, which should be denser and less fragile than first-bar chase logic.
invalidation_condition: Abort if delayed retests remain too sparse or if held retests still fail to continue enough after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout occurs, price retests the broken area instead of running away, then continuation resumes after the retest holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retests can provide better continuation entries than immediate breakout chasing, especially on volatile assets where first bars are often overextended.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because retest behavior is visible and expansion bursts are meaningful on this structurally different lane.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Retests may hold mechanically but continuation can still be too weak or too infrequent to justify the setup.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If retests trigger with acceptable density but follow-through is weak, reduce exit ambition before altering retest logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A smaller set of continuation trades than mean reversion ideas, but with cleaner second-leg bursts when right.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one major cross-lane show real density and positive post-cost continuation after held retests.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or held retests that repeatedly fail to expand.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest, hold, and directional participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: improved versus first-bar breakout logic because entry waits for hold rather than rare perfect breakout candles

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for delayed retest continuation on the structurally different asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest holds occur often enough and continuation pays after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Retests are too sparse or fail to expand.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Held retests that produce second-leg continuation bursts.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same delayed retest grammar works on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the retest continuation structure with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Idea only works on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner delayed retest continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same retest-and-hold idea survives on the faster adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h keeps density without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller delayed retest continuations.
